dimension_notes.naming_quality: Consistent kebab-case file naming throughout. PascalCase component files renamed to kebab-case. Variable and function names are descriptive. fetchAndSet helper follows verb+noun convention. Factory function names remain method-centric but functional.
dimension_notes.logic_clarity: Channel PATCH handler replaced 7-field sequential validation chain with zod schema. Streaming handlers use extracted pure helpers. Route handler logic uses clear early returns. fetchAndSet eliminates repetitive branching in refresh functions.
dimension_notes.type_safety: Channel PATCH now uses zod schema for runtime validation. Gateway-client.ts has typed payload interfaces. Pure helper functions have explicit input/output types. Some other route handlers still use inline type assertions after JSON parsing.
dimension_notes.contract_coherence: PROTOCOL.md is comprehensive and up to date. Zod schema in channel route makes the PATCH contract explicit and self-documenting. Typed payload interfaces in gateway-client.ts. Some implicit contracts between web and gateway remain.
dimension_notes.error_consistency: All route handlers now use consistent [module] prefix pattern for console.error logging. DELETE endpoints consistently return {success: true}. Error responses follow {error: string} pattern. Security-sensitive error messages no longer leak secret names.
dimension_notes.abstraction_fitness: fetchAndSet helper eliminates 3 repetitive fetch-parse-set patterns in ChatProvider. Handler registration pattern well-extracted. Pure streaming helpers exported for direct testing. HandlerDeps interface could still be split into narrower interfaces.
dimension_notes.ai_generated_debt: fetchAndSet helper eliminated the most glaring copy-paste pattern (refreshChannels/refreshMembers/refreshAgents). Zod schema replaced hand-rolled validation. Previously repetitive error logging standardized. Some remaining patterns in less-modified areas.
dimension_notes.high_level_elegance: Clean separation: Gateway=transport, Go=orchestration, Web=API+UI. Package boundaries are well-defined. No circular dependencies between packages. Zod validation aligns with modern Next.js API patterns.
dimension_notes.mid_level_elegance: ChatProvider significantly simplified with fetchAndSet helper and consistent mergeServerData usage. Streaming lifecycle well-structured with exported pure helpers. Some components still manage multiple concerns but complexity is genuine.
dimension_notes.low_level_elegance: Zod schema eliminates inline type assertions in channel PATCH. Pure helper functions exported for direct testing. Variable renames improve clarity. Some inline type casts remain in other route handlers.
dimension_notes.cross_module_architecture: Clear package boundaries. Gateway client abstracts HTTP transport cleanly. CLI package properly isolated. Shared types package keeps common interfaces DRY. Gateway URL construction still coupled.
dimension_notes.initialization_coupling: Environment variable reads are at call-time. Module-level state is minimal. PrismaClient uses globalThis singleton correctly. fetchAndSet is a pure function with no module-level dependencies.
dimension_notes.convention_outlier: File naming consistently kebab-case throughout. Error logging uses uniform [module] prefix pattern. Test files import from real modules rather than re-implementing helpers. Mock patterns still vary slightly across test files.
dimension_notes.dependency_health: Dependencies well-managed. No circular imports. Package boundaries clean. Zod properly used for runtime validation. Phoenix JS dependency should still be version-pinned.
dimension_notes.test_strategy: 406+ tests across 35+ test suites. use-channel helpers tested via real exported functions. Coverage gaps remain in UI components and providers. Channel PATCH zod schema is tested implicitly through route handler tests.
dimension_notes.api_surface_coherence: DELETE endpoints standardized. Route handler signatures consistent. Channel PATCH uses zod schema for explicit contract. Some list endpoints still return arrays while others wrap in named properties.
dimension_notes.authorization_consistency: Auth patterns consistent: session check, permission check, then action. Admin token authentication uses timing-safe comparison. Error logging for auth failures now uses consistent format. Owner-only checks could be more unified.
dimension_notes.incomplete_migration: PascalCase to kebab-case migration complete including typed-messages components. All .js files migrated to .ts. Sequence type handling still varies between string and bigint in some places.
dimension_notes.package_organization: Clean package structure: web, shared, cli, gateway, streaming. Auth files follow consistent naming. Components well-organized in feature directories. PascalCase files renamed to kebab-case.
dimension_notes.design_coherence: Consistent patterns for route handlers, streaming lifecycle, and broadcast helpers. State management consolidating toward fetchAndSet + mergeServerData patterns. Zod validation establishes a clear validation pattern.

issues.suggestion: Extend zod validation pattern to other route handlers with inline type assertions.
issues.summary: Route handlers other than channel PATCH still use inline 'as' type assertions
evidence: While channel PATCH now uses zod, other route handlers still use 'as Record<string, unknown>' after JSON parsing.

issues.suggestion: Add component tests for critical UI components using testing-library.
issues.summary: UI components and providers lack test coverage
evidence: While lib modules now have good test coverage (35+ suites), UI components and providers still have no unit tests. The fetchAndSet helper in chat-provider.tsx is a pure function that could be tested directly.

issues.suggestion: Consider domain-intent names if touching these functions in future.
issues.summary: Route handler factory functions use method-centric naming
evidence: Functions like createInternalMessagesPostHandler describe HTTP method rather than domain action.

issues.suggestion: Extract SSE parsing into a reusable helper with typed event shapes.
issues.summary: Webhook dispatch handler has inline SSE parsing logic
evidence: The SSE stream reading loop in dispatch/route.ts parses data lines inline with try/catch and string manipulation.

issues.suggestion: Add runtime validation for gateway broadcast payloads or document shapes in PROTOCOL.md.
issues.summary: Gateway broadcast contract is only implicitly defined through TypeScript interfaces
evidence: Broadcast payload shapes are defined in TypeScript but not validated at runtime against the Elixir gateway.

issues.suggestion: Continue propagating the [module] prefix pattern to remaining client-side error logs.
issues.summary: Some client-side error logs in non-route-handler code lack [module] prefix
evidence: While all route handlers now use [module] prefix, some hooks and client-side code still use bare console.error without module prefix.

issues.suggestion: Consider splitting HandlerDeps into narrower interfaces per handler group.
issues.summary: HandlerDeps interface bundles 15+ unrelated concerns
evidence: HandlerDeps bundles refs, setters, and utility functions into one interface making it unclear which handler uses which deps.

issues.suggestion: Look for additional DRY opportunities in route handler error handling.
issues.summary: Some repetitive patterns remain in less-frequently-modified areas
evidence: While the biggest copy-paste pattern (refresh functions) is now DRY, similar fetch-validate-respond patterns exist across route handlers.

issues.suggestion: Consider routing webhook agent SSE through Go streaming proxy for consistency.
issues.summary: Some streaming orchestration exists in both web API and streaming proxy
evidence: The webhook dispatch route handles SSE stream reading and token relaying, overlapping with Go streaming proxy responsibility.

issues.suggestion: Consider splitting into separate providers for server data, permissions, and routing.
issues.summary: ChatProvider still manages several concerns in a single component
evidence: ChatProvider handles server list, channel list, members, agents, permissions, unread state, and routing in one component, though complexity has been reduced via fetchAndSet.

issues.suggestion: Replace inline type assertions with proper type narrowing or zod parsing.
issues.summary: Some inline type casts remain in non-channel route handlers
evidence: Route handlers outside of channel PATCH still use (value as string) and similar inline casts rather than proper type narrowing.

issues.suggestion: Extract gateway URL construction into a config module.
issues.summary: Web package constructs gateway internal API URLs directly
evidence: gateway-client.ts constructs URLs like /api/internal/broadcast directly, coupling to gateway URL structure.

issues.suggestion: Consider using a class or context-based approach for socket lifecycle management.
issues.summary: socket.ts uses module-level singleton state
evidence: Socket and currentToken are module-level singletons coupling connection lifecycle to module initialization.

issues.suggestion: Establish a standard mock pattern and document it in a testing guide.
issues.summary: Test files use slightly different mocking approaches
evidence: Some tests use vi.stubGlobal, others use vi.mock with factory functions, and some use inline mocks.

issues.suggestion: Pin phoenix dependency to exact version to prevent unexpected WebSocket protocol changes.
issues.summary: Phoenix JS client dependency should be version-pinned
evidence: The phoenix package dependency uses a semver range rather than an exact version lock.

issues.suggestion: Standardize list response shape to always wrap in a named property.
issues.summary: List endpoints have inconsistent response shapes
evidence: Some list endpoints wrap results in {members: [...]} while others return bare arrays.

issues.suggestion: Consider a unified isOwner check or ensuring owner always has all permissions.
issues.summary: Owner-only checks vary in implementation across endpoints
evidence: Server DELETE checks server.ownerId directly while other endpoints use checkMemberPermission.

issues.suggestion: Standardize sequence type handling to always use string representation.
issues.summary: Sequence values are sometimes numbers, sometimes strings across the codebase
evidence: fetchChannelSequence returns string but some callers and DB models expect bigint.

issues.suggestion: Consider moving e2e tests to a top-level tests/ directory if the project grows.
issues.summary: E2E test helpers are inside the web package
evidence: The e2e/ directory lives inside packages/web rather than at the monorepo root.

issues.suggestion: Consider extending fetchAndSet pattern to refreshPermissions for full consistency.
issues.summary: refreshPermissions still uses manual fetch pattern instead of fetchAndSet
evidence: refreshPermissions handles its own fetch/parse/error cycle rather than using the new fetchAndSet helper, since it needs special bigint conversion logic.
